label: The mirror
sub: what I see when it’s just me
framing: Where I argue with myself. The quadrant I write in when I don’t know what I believe yet, where first drafts go to be held up to the light.

statement.text: prototype rapidly with AI & sticky notes

items.title: Designing AI products
items.dek: Humanity, in interfaces, is not about imitation.

items.title: How I create rapid prototypes
items.dek: Not despite AI. Because of it.

label: The practice
framing: What I kick off when nobody asked. Meetups, experiments, rituals I run on myself, the stuff that wouldn’t exist if I waited for permission.

items.title: Just learn through watching AI agents talk
Voice, gesture, or ambient sensing — anything but text
items.dek: You never had to type a prompt

items.title: Leave a message to your future self — AI delivers it at the right moment
items.dek: AI knew your future self better than today-you

items.title: Render your chain of thought as a live knowledge graph
items.dek: Thinking out loud had a shape

items.title: Opinionated responses — AI that pushes back, disagrees, and advocates for you
items.dek: AI had a point of view

items.title: Making work driven by passion, not FOMO
items.dek: Work could be passion-driven, not FOMO-driven

label: Paying attention
sub: paying attention to what I notice in other people
framing: Watching, listening, not-yet-acting. Most of what I’ve learned about design I’ve learned by watching people describe their work out loud.

items.title: Initiating events @Apple
items.dek: Build the community I wish existed?

label: The work
framing: The finished, shipped, collaborative stuff. Where thinking has to become a decision and other people have to live with it.

items.title: Shopping doesn’t have to start with a search
items.dek: Watch · create · purchase. Six business partners, one app.

items.title: AI doesn’t have to be complex
AI can be a design tool
items.dek: A working call agent built in a week of prompt engineering.

items.title: Design can be a research tool
AI doesn’t have to stay on a screen
items.dek: Embedding diagnostic AI into a clinical workflow.

items.title: AI can earn human trust
items.dek: Two papers on language, affiliation, and care.

items.title: Designing the generation–review loop
items.dek: A talk at Apple AIML on content generation, and the review process that keeps it honest.

items.title: Teaching is a design problem
items.dek: Head TA, Python and HCI. Office hours as a UX research method.
